title: Dr. Beckmann Starch & Easy Iron Spray | For a smooth & crisp finish on clothes | 500ml
features: For a smooth & crisp finish to clothes. Ideal for shirts etc.
Provides a natural tension to easily iron out creases
description: Product Description "Dr. Beckmann Spray Starch & Easy Iron combines two effective processes in one, making ironing easier than ever. The innovative formula creates natural tension, while ensuring easy smoothing of stubborn creases and wrinkles. You can even remove ironed-in creases, and the flattening of fibres helps prevent new wrinkles as well as repelling dirt. Giving your textiles perfect, controlled body and a crease-free finish for a well-groomed look and a comfortable feel. HOW TO USE IT: 1. Shake & spray the fabric evenly from approx. 20cm. Use more on shirt collars, cuffs and button strips until starched to your requirements 2. Iron as usual IMPORTANT INFORMATION: - Always follow fabric care instructions. - Suitable for all washable textiles made of natural fibres, synthetic fibres or blends. - Not suitable for wool, silk, silk-like synthetics (e.g. viscose, acetate) and dry clean only textiles. - Suitable for whites and coloureds. - Can be used on dry or damp clothing. - Suitable for all iron temperatures. - Does not stick or leave residues on the iron or fabric. - Warning: If sprayed on floor can make it slippery. If the spray nozzle becomes blocked, simply rinse with warm water (if necessary, screw off first). If ironing delicate or dark textiles, test first on an inconspicuous area.​ ECO CONTRIBUTION: - Made with 100% renewable electricity​ - 100% recycled bottle without cap & labels - Speeds up ironing process saving energy" Ingredients Tetramethylolglycoluril, Perfumes Safety Warning Warning: if sprayed on floor can make it slippery. If the spray nozzle becomes blocked, simply rinse with warm water (if necessary, screw off first). Always follow garment care label instructions. If ironing delicate or dark textiles, test first on a concealed area. Keep out of reach of children. See more
